Add support for validation cohorts

On a given field or type, there may be multiple "cohorts" of validation
which need to be processed together.  For example, a short-circuit
validation and a non-short-circuit for the same subfield.

The unnamed cohort (aka the default cohort) is first, followed by named
cohorts in order they are created.  Named cohorts are emitted as inline
functions, so early-return can be used.

This allows tags like k8s:subfield or k8s:item to generate cohorts
(named after the field's JSON name or the selector string,
respectively).

Subsequent commits will add support to those tags.

Co-Authored-by: Yongrui Lin <yongrlin@outlook.com>

Add ValidateObjectMetaWithOpts() to apimachinery

This new function does 1 main thing (changes how name validation works),
and the rest is future-proofing.  Specifically this changes the
signature of the name validation function.

Before: Name validation functions return `[]string` and apimachinery
logic wraps those in `field.Invalid()`.

Problem: This makes it hard to incrementally add declarative validation,
which needs access to potential name and generateName errors to set
origins properly.

After: New name validation functions return `field.ErrorList`, which can
be wrapped in trivial functions to do things like "mark as covered by
declarative" in just one place rather than for all types.

The "WithOpts" part of this gives us a new function name which isn't
horrible and is plausibly useful in the future.

Another notable change is that this no longer directly validates the
`generateName` field (but only on the new path -- existing code still
behaves the same).  From the code:

```
+   // generateName is not directly validated here. Types can have
+   // different rules for name generation, and the nameFn is for validating
+   // the post-generation data, not the input. In the past we assumed that
+   // name generation was always "append 5 random characters", but that's not
+   // NECESSARILY true. Also, the nameFn should always be considering the max
+   // length of the name, and it doesn't know enough about the name generation
+   // to do that. Also, given a bad generateName, the user will get errors
+   // for both the generateName and name fields. We will focus validation on
+   // the name field, which should give a better UX overall.
```

This also beefs up tests a bit.

Update CSR DV test to match RC style

Once we go broad, people will copy these.  Let's make them easy to debug
:)
Prefactor: Fix some bad tests

I think it is the right place - you want it as close to the "intention" as possible.  In this case you are validating annotation keys.  There should be no downside to it -- existing test ignore it and now you can convert more of them to use Origin (and remove the detail strings).

It looks like `TestValidatePodHostName()` uses ErrorMatcher...ByOrigin(), so any error which has an origin needs to be called out in the test.  This is why I wanted to make sure we take it one pice at a time - it does have a viral effect.

Since this now uses origin, you can can blank out the detail string (which is ultimately the goal of using origin). 

You can also change the value to `nil` because we don't match on value (it does `field.ErrorMatcher{}.ByType().ByField().ByOrigin().ByDetailSubstring()`)

So `field.Invalid(field.NewPath("spec.hostnameOverride"), nil, "").WithOrigin("format=k8s-long-name")` is the ideal result - as little specified as possible, but still high confidence that we got the expected error.

Then we can fix trhe actual error messages and not need to change 10000 tests.
same comment

Make ErrorMatcher more strict about multi-match

As discussed in a proposed PR, multi-match makes sense when origin is
specified and matched, but otherwise it feels "loose" to multi-match.
And in fact it was hiding bugs in declarative validation tests and real
bugs (in subsequent commits) in implementation.

Now we allow multi-match if and only if:
  1) ErrorMatcher.ByOrigin() was specified
  2) The origin string is not empty
  3) The multiple "got" errors are not exactly identical

This last part is key -- if some change to DV codegens logic which
(errantly) calls the same function twice, we want to know about it.

Temporary: Re-enable listmap uniqueness checks

Testing this exposed a bug -- we do this check for both the field AND
the type when the field is a typedef.  Now fixed to only emit a
uniqueness check at the site where listmap keys are defined.

I think this is an open question to be decided soon.  Actually a few questions.

* What level of abstraction do we want.  We know label keys are "qualified names" -- is that an OK detail to expose, or do we add another level of abstraction?
* Can we find any name better than "qualified name"? Please?
Either way, getting them all on the same value makes it that much easier to change them all later :)

We are wildly inconsistent about this

I don't think we want to be embedding per-volume-type fields into these neutral status types.  Why would we allow this but later say no to something like a GCE PD resource URL or an NFS canonicalized server path or ... ?

I was going to say "this is why KEPs get API reviewers' input", but I see that this KEP *did* get API review feedback 4 months ago, and it was not addressed.  :(

https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/pull/5375/files#r2152906859

I would assume the starting point for discussion would be something like:

```diff
diff --git staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go
index 361e05d0006..b91d9d3cdc1 100644
--- staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go
+++ staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go
@@ -3604,6 +3604,26 @@ type VolumeMountStatus struct {
 	// depending on the mount result.
 	// +optional
 	RecursiveReadOnly *RecursiveReadOnlyMode `json:"recursiveReadOnly,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,4,opt,name=recursiveReadOnly,casttype=RecursiveReadOnlyMode"`
+
+	// volumeStatus represents volume-type-specific status about the mounted
+	// volume.
+	VolumeStatus
+}
+
+// Represents the status of a mounted volume.
+// Exactly one of its members must be specified.
+type VolumeStatus struct {
+	// image represents an OCI object (a container image or artifact) pulled and mounted on the kubelet's host machine.
+	// +featureGate=ImageVolume
+	// +optional
+	Image *ImageVolumeStatus
+}
+
+type ImageVolumeStatus struct {
+	// ImageRef is the digest of the image used for this volume.
+	// It should have a value that's similar to the pod's status.containerStatuses[i].imageID.
+	// If the volume source is not an ImageVolume, this field will be empty.
+	ImageRef *string `json:"imageRef,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=imageRef"`
 }
 
 // RestartPolicy describes how the container should be restarted.
```
You want this to match the v1 structure -- you should not need to hand-write conversion code.

Help future readers comprehend this - I am not sure it's right.

If `features.ImageVolume` is enabled, then you don't wipe the status field?  What's the point of the 2nd gate?

These constructions are always coinfusing, so 2 gates is 4x more confusing.  Maybe this can be written more verbosely, like:

```
imageVolumeDisabled := !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ImageVolume)
imageVolumeDigestDisabled := imageVolumeDisabled || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ImageVolumeWithDigest)

if imageVolumeDigestDisabled && !imageVolumeWithDigestInUse(oldPodStatus) {
    dropImageVolumeWithDigest(podStatus)
}
```

?  These sorts of chained gates are confusing.  Can ImageVolumeWithDigest be set without ImageVolume?
The correct fieldpath would be something like `fldPath.Child("containerStatuses").Index(i).Child("volumeMounts").Index(j).Child("volumeStatus")`

Usually like: 

```
        for i, containerStatus := range newPod.Status.ContainerStatuses {
            fldPath := fldPath.Child("containerStatuses").Index(i)
			for j, volumeMountStatus := range containerStatus.VolumeMounts {
                fldPath := fldPath.Child("volumeMounts").Index(j)
                allErrs = append(allErrs, validateMaxOneFieldSet(volumeMountStatus.VolumeStatus, fldPath.Child("volumeStatus"))...)
```

Do we really need a reflect based implementation for this?  It's significantly slower than just writing a trivial function for it, and a trivial function doesn't demand a unit test of its own (which this does, I think).

`validateMaxOneVolumeStatus(&volumeMountStatus.VolumeStatus)`

This should probably have an error parallel to the spec: "may not specify more than 1 volume type".  It doesn't really even need a list of populated fields.

When declarative validation is enabled, this will be automatic :)
At most one, not exactly, since it is used as an embedded struct not an embedded pointer.  E.g. an emptyDir volume does not has a specific status.
ImageVolumeWithDigest ?
If the volume source is not an ImageVolume this whole struct is disallowed, right?

Do we really have no spec for it?

Is `""` a valid value?  Is `"x"` valid?  Is `"Four score and seven years ago, ..." valid?  I suspect you want to spec it as non-empty and limit the length to something like 128 bytes?

If the ImageVolumeWithDigest gate is enabled and someone uses an image volume and their kubelet is smart enough to set `.status.containeStatuses[i].volumeMounts[j].image`  to a non-nil value, is it OK for this field to be absent?     I don't think so - it should be `+required` and does not need to be a pointer.

For discussion: Is this redundant or useful?  "required" means something different to openapi (the field was specified, but `field=[]` is allowed) than it means in DV (the field must have at least 1 element).  This makes me feel like having both is useful, since the tags can be used to generate different things.  OTOH, we could simply assert that DV 'required' always means both 'required' and 'minItems=1'.

This commit adds a call to `validate.MinItems(..., 1)` which is totally redundant, but avoiding it requires a little more scaffolding.  IMO it's not very important right now.

This commit also elides calls to `validate.MinItems(..., 0)` - lists cannot have less than 0 items, obviously

xref https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/136976#discussion_r2906508641
